summary: Validated Priority D multi-agent workflow surfaces and regressions on public preview. Agents tab/control panel render correctly, stored workflow GET contract is valid, routing/catalog/import regressions pass, but live agent-workflow execution currently fails due exhausted upstream LLM budget.

critical.issue: POST fails with 400: 'Failed to generate chat completion ... Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 2.035857600000001, Max budget: 2.0'. This blocks live Planner/Evidence/Risk/Reporter execution.

minor.issue: Intermittent public endpoint instability observed as transient 502/connection abort during repeated test creation calls; retries required.

ui_bugs.issue: Tab trigger elements (e.g., Agents) are interactive but do not expose data-testid; automation required text fallback selector.

integration_issues.flow: Run Detail -> Agents -> Run multi-agent workflow
integration_issues.issue: Workflow status transitions to failed and shows budget-exceeded backend error; reporter update for new execution cannot complete.

action_items: Restore/replace valid LLM budget/key for configured universal-auth providers so POST /api/runs/{run_id}/agent-workflow can execute end-to-end again.
After key/budget fix, re-run Priority D live POST workflow tests to validate planner-first, parallel middle stage, reporter completion, and current-run memory behavior.
Optionally add data-testid attributes to tab triggers in shared Tabs component for full testability compliance.
critical_code_review_comments: agent_runtime_service.handoff_summary says Evidence Normalizer hands off to Risk Reviewer, but workflow executes evidence_normalizer and risk_reviewer in parallel; wording may mislead operators about real dependency timing.
Current workflow retrieval returns latest execution only; a failed latest run can hide previously successful step outputs in the UI context without quick fallback visibility.
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_ prefixed targets/runs via API during Priority D and scanner-import regression tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: UI Agents tab/control panel is present and wired. Existing run 3df373f0-8ac1-4efb-82b6-07a0deb135db still contains prior successful multi-agent artifacts/report draft, but new workflow launches now fail due LLM budget exhaustion. Non-LLM regressions (routing policy management, scanner import text/json, OpenRouter catalog refresh) passed.
rca of the issue: Reproduction: 1) POST /api/runs/3df373f0-8ac1-4efb-82b6-07a0deb135db/agent-workflow with openai/anthropic in direct mode, or click Run multi-agent workflow in Agents tab. 2) Response/status becomes failed with message 'Budget has been exceeded'. Root cause: upstream LLM account quota/budget exhaustion in provider runtime path (not local schema/router logic). Mitigation: replenish/rotate provider credentials or budget, then rerun Priority D e2e checks.
